entry.title: Elasticsearch on NetApp HCI H615C with EF-Series EF280

link.title: Elasticsearch on NetApp HCI H615C with EF-Series EF280

entry.summary: Summary Test results Configuration Elasticsearch data protection with EF-Series Workload description Performance-related observations Client Elastisearch VMs Network EF280 storage performance HCI H615C vs H410C, Bare Metal vs VMs NetApp HCI server-to-EF-Series array ratio Management OS (VM) deployment Elasticsearch deployment Storage management Monitoring Advantage of disaggregated HCI

entry.title: BeeGFS on NetApp HCI with EF280 for high-speed file access from Virtual Machines

link.title: BeeGFS on NetApp HCI with EF280 for high-speed file access from Virtual Machines

entry.summary: Summary Why and when run BeeGFS in NetApp HCI VMs Aren’t shared file systems complex to deploy and manage How do automation and virtualization help Performance Considerations What next References Update (Jan 05, 2020)

entry.title: Virtualized Splunk on NetApp HCI and EF Series iSCSI storage

link.title: Virtualized Splunk on NetApp HCI and EF Series iSCSI storage

entry.summary: Things are getting interesting Does EF-Series for Splunk on NetApp HCI make sense How does it work How does that compare with other HCI Can we get to the point and see those bonnie++ results How hard is it to manage NetApp HCI-attached E/EF-Series iSCSI arrays This comparison doesn’t apply to my situation Conclusion Resources

entry.title: DiskSpd performance from NetApp HCI VM to EF280

link.title: DiskSpd performance from NetApp HCI VM to EF280

entry.summary: Summary Why test EF280 performance from a VM VMFS vs. Raw Devices Manageability and iSCSI Cost of an All Flash backup tier Reference Appendix: Configuration and DiskSpd logs NTFS striped over two VMDK/Datastores ReFS on Raw Device ReFS Striped across four Raw Devices

entry.title: Adapter, network and VLAN isolation on NetApp HCI

link.title: Adapter, network and VLAN isolation on NetApp HCI

entry.summary: This comes up every so often:

entry.title: Protect your Kubernetes applications and data with Kasten and SolidFire

link.title: Protect your Kubernetes applications and data with Kasten and SolidFire

entry.summary: Rancher and other Kubernetes users want to protect their applications and data. NetApp HCI and its underlying SolidFire storage are used by NetApp Trident to provide a CSI-compatible API to Kubernetes-based orchestrators.

entry.title: SolidFire PowerShell Tools, Python CLI and SDK pip packages v1.7

link.title: SolidFire PowerShell Tools, Python CLI and SDK pip packages v1.7

entry.summary: This is a nice Christmas present as far as I am concerned, because it’s been a while since SolidFire PowerShell tools, sfcli and their respective SDKs v1.5 have been released.

entry.title: Rancher Kubernetes Layer-7 NLB/Ingress with NGINX Plus and Let’s Encrypt

link.title: Rancher Kubernetes Layer-7 NLB/Ingress with NGINX Plus and Let's Encrypt

entry.summary: tl;dr Introduction and requirements Resources Deploy Linux VM and N+ Create DNS entries, get TLS certificates, configure NGINX Prepare DNS and open public firewall (port TCP/443) TLS with Let’s Encrypt Install Rancher Servers Configure N+ Upstream (Rancher) servers on different network kubectl X.509 ‘certificate signed by unknown authority’ error Use N+ to reverse-proxy multiple Rancher clusters or services Use N+ to reverse-proxy other API or service endpoints Next steps

entry.title: Get NetApp Hybrid Cloud Control logs via the HCC API

link.title: Get NetApp Hybrid Cloud Control logs via the HCC API

entry.summary: Hybrid Cloud Control (HCC) is a group of services - management plane, if you will - for out-of-band management of NetApp HCI, SolidFire and eSDS. Currently it’s a bunch of containers that runs in a VM commonly called mNode (short for Management Node).

entry.title: How much slower is iSCSI vs. Fibre Channel

link.title: How much slower is iSCSI vs. Fibre Channel

entry.summary: If you search for “iSCSI vs FC performance” it’s not easy to find a recent like-for-like comparison from enterprise vendors. I’ve been puzzled by this and I have some theories as to why, but regardless - the fact is there aren’t that many comparisons out there.
